_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects (established W10); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.

resolution.summary: Wave-2 QA for the W11a UI batch (EM-086 run browser API/UI, EM-093 frozen-snapshot feed, EM-094 narrator + digest, EM-097 SocialGraph cleanup, animal payload.place). Delivered: (1) the W10-QA-1 strict xfail pin in SocialGraph.test.tsx flipped to a plain passing `it` — wave 1's EM-097 captured-instance cleanup made it pass, turning the pin RED exactly as designed; comment updated to record the fix. (2) backend/tests/test_w11a.py: 18 new tests covering /api/runs RunRow conformance (newest-first, is_active from the loop-held run NEVER the status column, aggregates, legacy/malformed config_summary degradation), run_id scoping on the read endpoints incl. the parametrized unknown-run 404 across all seven, narrator mode (off-by-default zero cost, one summary per window with the contract payload, failure emits nothing + loop keeps ticking, unavailable-profile skip), and animal_action payload.place (wander carries the destination; non-move actions carry no place key). (3) 43 new frontend tests in 5 files: storySoFar digest (11), archiveAgents (8), inspectorApi run_id threading + /api/runs parsing (9), RunBrowser (10), EventFeed frozen-snapshot logic (5). All four validation gates re-run green; zero src files modified; no new bugs found.

contract_conformance.notes: Verified against api.openapi.yaml v1.3.0 + event-log.md v1.2.0 + frontend-inspector.md v1.2.0 §8/§9 with native pins on every sharp edge: /api/runs is newest-first with the full RunRow key set; is_active is true ONLY for the loop-held run — a crashed run whose stored status is still 'running' reads is_active=false at the repo layer AND renders the ARCHIVED chip in RunBrowser (status is secondary text only); config_summary is a projection ({agents,seed?}, never the world blob) degrading legacy/malformed config_json to agents=[]; end_run stamps ended_at (the v1.3.0 migration); omitted run_id = active run byte-identically, explicit run_id scopes events/turns/snapshots/replay to THAT run (replay base.state comes from the run's own snapshot state_json per EM-086 note 2), unknown run_id = 404 {"detail":"unknown run"} on all seven read endpoints (parametrized); narrator_summary matches note 1 exactly (actor_type system, actor_id narrator, no inherited turn_id, payload {from_tick,to_tick,profile,routed_via?}, one per every_n_ticks window, failure emits nothing, disabled = zero provider calls); wander animal_action carries destination payload.place and non-moves don't — source-verified that wander is the ONLY location-changing animal action, so the action=='wander' guard is complete. Frontend: inspectorApi threads run_id iff runId is set (URL-asserted per fetcher), runs() null-on-failure vs []-on-empty is pinned, storySoFar/archiveAgents match §9/§8 semantics incl. labeled-empty degradation. Two conformance observations, both judged in-contract: (a) a narrator window whose previous call is still in flight is skipped without queueing — consistent with 'at most once per window / never retried'; (b) list_runs computes is_active as rid==loop-held-id without re-checking MAX(id); in every reachable state the loop-held run IS MAX(id) (start_run is loop-only), so the two definitions coincide.

test_coverage.notes: Backend +18 (206 total): RunRow shape/order/aggregates/is_active/config_summary (4), run_id scoping incl. the 7-endpoint 404 parametrization (9), narrator on/off/failure/unavailable (4 — with per-window call counting on a fake provider so 'zero extra LLM calls' is asserted literally), animal payload.place move/non-move (2, plus persisted-row verification). Frontend +43 (106 total / 16 files): storySoFar 11 (empty shape, death-tick recovery + out-of-window null-tick ordering, active-rule count/newest text/vote-in-progress, projects, drama newest-first precedence + per-register labels + calm-history null, narrator pickup + labeled off-state), archiveAgents 8 (config seeding, event discovery, died/moved/profile attribution, actor-type filtering + missing-actor_type tolerance, turn_start-anchored economy with delta fold and re-anchor), api 9, RunBrowser 10 (incl. the status-'running'-zombie ARCHIVED rendering and view/return-to-live semantics), EventFeed freeze 5. EM-093 coverage honesty: the freeze mechanism is a literal row-set snapshot, so the unit tests assert the RENDERED ROW SET (arrivals mutate nothing, seq-set 'X new' counting immune to the 200-cap trim, pill/scroll-top thaw, filter-change re-pin) by driving the component's own onScroll with a per-element scrollTop stub — real pixel viewport stability under browser layout is NOT unit-testable in jsdom and remains gate-3 Playwright territory ('50 arrivals, fixed viewport' regression bar). Carried gap from W10: useSimulation internals (synthetic-seq, reconnect) still closure-bound and untested; useArchiveHistory's keyset backfill loop is similarly hook-internal (its data path is covered via the api.ts + archiveAgents tests).

security.notes: No new runtime surface from this wave-2 (test files only; vite build re-verified clean and byte-equivalent in warning profile). Wave-1 surface reviewed while testing: run_id is validated with a parameterized SELECT (run_exists) before any scoped read — no injection path, unknown ids fail closed with 404; list_runs uses parameterized SQL with a single LEFT JOIN; config_summary deliberately projects only {agents:[{name,profile}],seed} and never the full config blob (no accidental key/path leakage into the run browser — asserted by test_list_runs: 'world' absent from the projection); narrator calls route only to configured profiles via the existing Router availability gate (never to a missing/keyless profile — pinned by the unavailable-profile test) and its prompt contains only world-internal event text. No real API keys anywhere in the suites (MockProvider/counting fakes; conftest pins EM_DB_PATH=':memory:' so TestClient runs can't touch live run history). Local-lab posture (unauthenticated god endpoints) unchanged and accepted by design.

regression_risk.notes: Zero src files modified (ownership honored). The only change to existing tests is the sanctioned EM-097 flip (it.fails → it, comment updated). All 63 pre-existing frontend tests pass unmodified alongside the 43 new ones; backend 188 pre-existing pass alongside the 18 new. Validations: pytest 206/206; vitest 106/106 (16 files); `npx tsc -b` clean (new tests type-checked under the strict app tsconfig); `npm run build` clean (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only); eslint clean on every new/changed test file. New tests are deterministic and offline: no network, no real timers needed (background narrator/animal tasks are drained explicitly per turn), seeded/scripted providers, jsdom scrollTop stubbed per-element with a real getter/setter so the component's own pin-to-top write keeps working.

frontend.changed_existing: src/inspector/SocialGraph.test.tsx ONLY — the W10-QA-1 strict xfail pin flipped to a plain `it` (EM-097 fixed it; the pin turned RED as designed); header + test comments updated to record the captured-instance-cleanup fix

test_results.frontend_verification: cd web && npx tsc -b — clean; npm run build — clean (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only); npx eslint on all new/changed test files — clean

issues.title: W10-QA-1 closed by wave 1 (EM-097): SocialGraph's pause-on-unmount now actually fires
issues.detail: Wave 1 replaced the dead read-the-ref-at-teardown cleanup with a captured-instance cleanup keyed on `ready` (SocialGraph.tsx:149-157): when `ready` flips true the graph mounts in the same commit, the effect captures the live instance into a closure-held local that survives React 18's commit-phase ref detach, and `fg?.pauseAnimation()` runs at unmount and whenever `ready` drops. The strict `it.fails` xfail pin flipped RED on that change, exactly as designed, and is now a plain passing regression test (SocialGraph.test.tsx). Ledger entry EM-097 can be closed.

issues.title: Narrator skips (not queues) a window whose previous call is still in flight
issues.detail: TickLoop._maybe_schedule_narrator returns early when an earlier window's narrator task is still pending ('skip, no queueing'), so a narrator provider slower than every_n_ticks × tick_interval produces FEWER than one summary per window. Judged in-contract (event-log.md v1.2.0 note 1: 'at most once per window', 'never retried', 'the loop must never stall on it') and the right free-scale call; recorded so nobody mistakes sparse summaries on a slow profile for a bug.

issues.title: EM-093 unit coverage is row-set-level; pixel-level viewport stability stays on gate 3 (carried, by design)
issues.detail: jsdom performs no layout, so the unit tests prove the freeze mechanism's actual contract (the rendered row set does not change while un-pinned; seq-set 'X new' counting; thaw/re-pin paths) by driving the component's onScroll with a per-element scrollTop getter/setter — they cannot prove the browser keeps the scroll position pixel-stable across 50 arrivals. That regression bar ('50 arrivals, fixed viewport') is explicitly the orchestrator's gate-3 Playwright verification per W11A_BUILD.md; no flaky scroll simulation was built in its place.

issues.title: useSimulation internals (synthetic-seq, reconnect) and useArchiveHistory's backfill loop remain unit-untested (carried from W10)
issues.detail: Unchanged finding from the W10 report: the synthetic-seq counter and reconnect/backoff are closures inside useSimulation; W11a's useArchiveHistory keyset backfill is similarly hook-internal (mounting it would need a mocked fetch world). Their pure data paths ARE now covered indirectly (inspectorApi URL construction + archiveAgents projection). If a future wave touches these hooks, extract the pure pieces (nextSyntheticSeq, the backfill page-fold) into lib modules and add the trivial tests then.

gate_decision.reason: All W11a wave-2 QA objectives delivered and every gate is green: backend 206/206 (18 new tests pinning /api/runs RunRow conformance, run_id scoping with fail-closed 404s, narrator-mode cost/emission/failure semantics, and the exact-animal-replay payload.place), frontend 106/106 across 16 files (43 new tests for the storySoFar digest, archiveAgents, run_id fetcher threading, RunBrowser incl. the is_active-not-status ACTIVE chip, and the EM-093 frozen-snapshot row-set contract), tsc -b + vite build + eslint clean, zero src modifications, and the EM-097 pin flipped from xfail to a real passing regression test. No new bugs found against the v1.3.0/v1.2.0 contracts; the four recorded items are one resolution (W10-QA-1/EM-097), one in-contract behavior note (narrator window skip), and two honest coverage boundaries that gate 3's Playwright pass owns (pixel-level scroll stability) or that carry forward by design (hook-internal logic). GREEN to proceed to gate 3 (orchestrator live verification).
